title: eNor Taps Matera To Provide Seamless Cross-Border Payments
excerpt: Matera, a leading banking software provider, announced a partnership with eNor Securities, a fintech offering an investment exchange for digital assets and a marketplace for
body:
Matera, a leading banking software provider, announced a partnership with eNor Securities, a fintech offering an investment exchange for digital assets and a marketplace for tokenized assets. This agreement will enable eNor to provide seamless cross-border payments from fiat to bitcoin and stablecoins.

For over 30 years, Matera has been a trusted provider of technology solutions for fintechs and financial institutions. The company's Pix instant payments solution processes over 5 billion transactions a year for over 230 banks and fintechs, including two of the top three banks in the world and over one-third of all banks in Brazil. Nearly half of those transactions are initiated using their QR Code payment technology.

Regulated by El Salvador's forward-thinking Bitcoin Law and Digital Assets Issuance Law, eNor Securities provides an array of services, from Bitcoin custody to asset tokenization. eNor is also a first mover in Real World Assets tokenization, offering commodity tokenization solutions through its partnership with E-Grains.

"By partnering with eNor, Matera is furthering the development of a more innovative, global payment ecosystem. Tokenized assets and blockchain-based technology will fuel the future of finance. Matera is at the forefront of this transformation, integrating our Digital Twin and Wallet-as-a-Service technologies to create a seamless bridge between traditional banking and the digital frontier," said Carlos Netto, Co-founder and CEO of Matera.

"This is an exciting milestone for eNor and Matera, and exemplifies an evolving financial ecosystem where digital asset technology and traditional banking infrastructure converge. We're committed to building a more inclusive and efficient global economy. This partnership marks a significant step forward in that mission," said Rodrigo Mendes, CEO of eNor Securities.

The partnership leverages El Salvador's cryptocurrency-friendly regulatory environment, Matera's Digital Twin and QR Code Payment technologies, and eNor's rigorous KYC/AML compliance standards. Through Matera's Wallet-as-a-Service, the solution enables seamless integration across banking and fintech ecosystems, creating a tokenized payment network.

The solution will be available for eNor corporate and retail customers in September.
